help.overview: Debris Screening runs a conjunction assessment for the selected satellite against the rest of the catalog. It finds every object that comes within a screening box you define around the satellite during the chosen time window, then ranks the encounters by probability of collision.
help.imgAlt: The Debris Screening menu with time window and screening box options
help.imgCaption: Set the time window and RIC screening box, then screen the selected satellite against the catalog.
help.box: The screening volume is defined in RIC coordinates relative to the satellite: U (Radial) is up and down in orbit altitude, V (In-track) is along the direction of flight, and W (Cross-track) is side to side. The radial size matters most: most encounters separate cleanly in altitude, so a tight U with a wider V and W is the usual choice. Draw Box renders the volume around the satellite in the 3D view; Clear Box removes it.
help.results: Each row of the results table is one predicted conjunction: Secondary is the other object, TCA is the time of closest approach, Miss (km) is the total miss distance with its R, I, and C components, Pc is the probability of collision, and Risk classifies it as Critical, High, Medium, or Low. Click a row to select the secondary object and jump the clock to the TCA; the menu stays open so you can step through events. Export CSV saves the table.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a satellite; the bottom icon is disabled until one is selected.
  2. Open Debris Screening from the bottom menu. The satellite's SCC number fills in automatically.
  3. Pick a Time Window (1 to 72 hours) and adjust the U, V, and W box sizes if needed.
  4. Click Screen for Debris. The results panel opens with the conjunctions found, ranked for review.
  5. Click any row to inspect that encounter at its time of closest approach.
help.tip1: Longer windows and bigger boxes find more encounters but take longer to compute; start with the defaults and widen only if you need more coverage.
help.tip2: Clicking a row preserves your playback state: if the clock was paused it stays paused at the TCA.
help.tip3: Risk levels are thresholds on Pc: above 1e-4 is Critical, above 1e-6 is High, and above 1e-8 is Medium.
